name: Where-Object

options.name: -CContains
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects from a collection if the property value of the object is an exact match for the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CEQ
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is the same as the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CGE
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is greater than or equal to the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CGT
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is greater than the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CIn
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value includes the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CLE
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is less-than or equal to the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CLike
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value matches a value that includes wildcard characters (`*`). This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CLT
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is less-than the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CMatch
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value matches the specified regular expression. This operation is case-sensitive. When the input is a single object, the matched value is saved in the `$Matches` automatic variable.

options.name: -CNE
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is different than the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CNotContains
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value of the object isn't an exact match for the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CNotIn
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value isn't an exact match for the specified value. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CNotLike
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value doesn't match a value that includes wildcard characters. This operation is case-sensitive.

options.name: -CNotMatch
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value doesn't match the specified regular expression. This operation is case-sensitive. When the input is a single object, the matched value is saved in the `$Matches` automatic variable.

name: -Contains
-IContains
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if any item in the property value of the object is an exact match for the specified value.

name: -EQ
-IEQ
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is the same as the specified value.

options.name: -FilterScript
options.description: Specifies the script block that's used to filter the objects. Enclose the script block in braces (`{}`).

name: -GE
-IGE
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is greater than or equal to the specified value.

name: -GT
-IGT
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is greater than the specified value.

name: -In
-IIn
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value matches any of the specified values. For example:

options.name: -InputObject
options.description: Specifies the objects to filter. You can also pipe the objects to `Where-Object`.

options.name: -Is
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is an instance of the specified .NET type. Enclose the type name in square brackets.

options.name: -IsNot
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value isn't an instance of the specified .NET type.

name: -LE
-ILE
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is less than or equal to the specified value.

name: -Like
-ILike
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value matches a value that includes wildcard characters (`*`).

name: -LT
-ILT
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is less than the specified value.

name: -Match
-IMatch
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value matches the specified regular expression. When the input is a single object, the matched value is saved in the `$Matches` automatic variable.

name: -NE
-INE
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value is different than the specified value.

options.name: -Not
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property doesn't exist or has a value of `$null` or `$false`.

name: -NotContains
-INotContains
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if none of the items in the property value is an exact match for the specified value.

name: -NotIn
-INotIn
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value isn't an exact match for any of the specified values.

name: -NotLike
-INotLike
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects if the property value doesn't match a value that includes wildcard characters (`*`).

name: -NotMatch
-INotMatch
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets objects when the property value doesn't match the specified regular expression. When the input is a single object, the matched value is saved in the `$Matches` automatic variable.

options.name: -Property
options.description: Specifies the name of an object property. The parameter name, Property , is optional.

options.name: -Value
options.description: Specifies a property value. The parameter name, Value , is optional. This parameter accepts wildcard characters when used with the following comparison parameters:

description: Selects objects from a collection based on their property values.
